title: RMS Language Definition
description: Every RMS command, its attributes, preprocessor directives, and runtime control keywords, for DE — section membership, argument shapes, mutual exclusivity. Powers the Phase 2 parser, Monaco autocomplete/highlighting (src/editor/aoe2RmsLanguage.ts's COMMANDS/ATTRIBUTES lists should eventually be generated from this file instead of hand-duplicated), and Breakdown's command/attribute dropdowns. Attributes are defined once in the top-level attributes[] array and referenced by name from each command that accepts them, rather than duplicated per command — a command's own attributes[] field is just a list of names pointing into that array.

sections.description: The section headers a script is divided into (e.g. <PLAYER_SETUP>), in generation order. Written without the angle brackets here.

section.description: One of the top-level sections[] entries, e.g. "LAND_GENERATION".

kind.description: block = opens a { } body (e.g. create_land { ... }). standalone = a single statement with inline arguments and no body (e.g. base_terrain GRASS).

arguments.description: Inline arguments taken by this command itself (both kinds can have these — a block command's MaxHeight-style argument, or a standalone command's whole argument list).

attributes.description: Names of entries in the top-level attributes[] array valid inside this command's { } block. Only meaningful when kind is "block"; omit or leave empty for standalone commands.

mutexWith.description: Names of other attributes that cannot be used together with this one in the same block, e.g. land_percent vs. number_of_tiles.

commands.name: create_land
commands.description: Creates a land mass on the map.

attributes.name: land_percent

arguments.name: percent
arguments.description: Share of the map this land should occupy.

attributes.description: Sets land size as a percentage of total map area, instead of an absolute tile count.

directives.name: #const
directives.description: Defines a named integer constant usable anywhere a number is expected.

controlKeywords.name: start_random
controlKeywords.description: Begins a block of percent_chance-weighted alternatives, closed by end_random.
